title: AI Operating System for Consulting Firms
metaDescription: Install and run an AI Operating System for your consulting firm. We handle lead reply, engagement kickoff, proposal follow-up, and status reporting so partners can spend hours where the rate is.
excerpt: An AI Operating System is the coordination layer that runs the work around the actual consulting. Here is what we install, in what order, and what done looks like.

sections.intro:
Consulting firms run on a thin operating layer that lives in three places: the partner's head, scattered Google Docs, and Slack threads. New engagements scope themselves from scratch every time. Client follow-up depends on whoever remembers. Status reports take a Friday afternoon. Most of that is not consulting work. It is the coordination tax around the actual thinking.

An AI Operating System (AIOS) is the layer that runs the coordination so consultants can spend their hours where the rate is. For a consulting firm, that means every inbound lead is answered in under 60 seconds with your booking link, every new engagement kicks off the same way, open proposals get followed up automatically, and weekly client updates draft themselves from live notes and calendar data.

This page is the overview. Below is what we install, in what order, and what done looks like.
sections.fourCFit:
An AIOS is built on four layers, installed in this order:

  • Context: the system knows the firm. Your service lines, the way you write to clients, your engagement structure, the methodology you bring. The same context every junior asks for in their first month.
  • Connections: the system reaches your tools. Your inbox and calendar, your CRM (HubSpot, Pipedrive, Copper), your time tracker, your file storage, your project tool. Live data, no manual copying.
  • Capabilities: done-for-you workflows. Reply to a new inquiry. Send a discovery deck. Draft a status update. Chase an open proposal. Summarize a call.
  • Cadence: it runs on a schedule. A morning brief on partner desks at 7am. A weekly status to each engagement on Friday. A proposal follow-up sequence that runs without anyone setting a reminder.

For most firms we work with, Context and Connections are the unlock. Once those are in place, Capabilities ship fast and Cadence makes them durable.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
Done for a consulting firm typically looks like this in the first 90 days:

  • Every inbound lead gets a personalized 60-second reply with your booking link. See 60-Second Lead Response for Consulting Firms.
  • Every new engagement kicks off the same way, with the same intake, deck, and SOW touchpoints. See Client Onboarding Automation for Consulting Firms.
  • Open proposals get followed up automatically on day 3, 7, and 14, in your voice. See Proposal and Quote Follow-Up for Consulting Firms.
  • Weekly status updates draft from live calendar, notes, and time data. The partner reviews and sends. See Automated Client Reporting for Consulting Firms.

You own everything. Every prompt, connection, and automation lives in your accounts.
sections.howWeInstall:
Three phases:

  1. Audit, free, about 10 minutes. Score your AI readiness across the Four Cs and surface the three highest-leverage automations for your firm. Run the audit.
  2. Install, 10 business days. We wire up your tools, capture your firm's voice and methodology, and ship your first working automations. The free 60-Second Lead Engine is usually first because partners feel the impact in the first booked call.
  3. Operate, ongoing. We run it, watch it, fix it, and ship one new automation per week, measured against your kickoff baseline. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
For a typical consulting firm in the 3 to 25 staff range, we plan against this baseline:

  • Recover 40 or more hours a month inside the team's calendar, or we keep working free until you do.
  • Lead response time from hours to under 60 seconds.
  • Proposal follow-up coverage from inconsistent to 100 percent.
  • Status reporting time per client down by 70 to 90 percent.

We do not promise close-rate lift. We promise recovered hours and the SLA.
sections.faq:
Who owns the system?

You do. Every prompt, connection, and automation lives in your accounts. If you ever stop working with us, you keep all of it.

What if something breaks?

We monitor every workflow and fix it. You get alerts and a clear log of every run, no silent failures.

How fast until ROI?

First automations are live within 10 business days. We measure recovered hours against your kickoff baseline over the following 30 days. The Lead Engine alone usually pays back within the first booked call.
